features.summary: Xilinx reported Q2 fiscal 2021 revenue of $767 million, up 5% sequentially and above guidance midpoint. Data Center Group (DCG) achieved a record quarter crossing $100 million for the first time, driven by cloud AI clusters and Solarflare products. The company secured a major SmartNIC design win with a Tier 1 hyperscaler expected to generate over $100 million annually by FY 2024. Core markets showed recovery with automotive rebounding and aerospace & defense setting records.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Q3 revenue between $750-800 million, up 1% sequentially at midpoint. DCG is expected to decline from record Q2 levels due to some order pull-ins from trade restrictions, while WWG should increase as 5G deployments continue. The company remains confident in double-digit growth for DCG over the long term and expects to exceed 4G revenue run rates in wireless despite near-term headwinds. Management emphasized continued platform transformation and ecosystem development with 43,000 Vitis downloads and growing customer engagements.
features.risks: Trade restrictions continue to create quarterly volatility, with Huawei completely removed from outlook across all business segments. The DCG business remains lumpy quarter-to-quarter due to its consolidated customer base and early scaling phase. COVID-19 impacts persist with most employees working from home, though supply chain and development activities continue largely uninterrupted. Competitive pressures are increasing in SmartNIC markets with new entrants like Qualcomm announcing macro base station products.
features.key_drivers: 5G deployment acceleration is driving RFSoC adoption with Tier 1 OEM production ramps in North America and expanding global design win pipeline. Zynq-based revenues grew 28% sequentially to 22% of total company revenue, demonstrating strong SoC momentum across target markets. The automotive market is recovering with multiple ADAS design wins including Subaru and Continental partnerships. Open-RAN initiatives present significant future opportunities with the company positioned as a technology front-runner for massive MIMO applications.
